commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens the root launcher optional-import guard to recognize Bun raw-specifier module-miss messages and adds a launcher probe test for direct versus transitive misses.

PR surface: Tests +48, Other +6. Total +54 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. A Bun 1.3.x global install or launcher fixture that omits dist/warning-filter.* should hit current main's ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND gate and rethrow the raw Bun miss; I did not execute that live path in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer handling and contributor-provided real Bun proof are needed; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply from this review.

target.title: fix(cli): handle Bun optional import misses
target.body:
Fixes #86198

## Summary
- Recognize Bun-style direct module-miss messages in the launcher optional-import guard.
- Keep Node direct misses supported through `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, URL, and absolute-path checks.
- Preserve the transitive-miss behavior so missing dependencies from `dist/entry.js` are still surfaced instead of being treated as missing OpenClaw build output.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 23 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check openclaw.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json openclaw.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts`

Note: commit is SSH-signed locally and verified with `git log --show-signature -1` after rebasing onto current `main`.
